findings.title: Storage and manifest path escape
findings.evidence: Traversal and Windows-ambiguous URI regression tests failed before remediation.
findings.remediation: Canonical component validation, root resolution, symlink rejection, bounded manifests.

findings.title: Training license gate trusted attacker-controlled manifest fields
findings.evidence: A forged approved/commercial manifest reached the export decision.
findings.impact: Restricted data could enter commercial training.
findings.remediation: Use authoritative catalog decisions and verify image checksum and storage URI.

findings.title: Unsafe local model boundary
findings.evidence: Model paths were not uniformly rooted and safe tensor loading was not mandatory.
findings.impact: Malicious model replacement or unsafe deserialization.
findings.remediation: Model-root enforcement, no symlinks, local_files_only, trust_remote_code false, safetensors and weights_only.

findings.title: Dataset downloader SSRF and redirect/proxy exposure
findings.evidence: Private HTTP targets were reached before validation.
findings.impact: Access to local/private services through controlled dataset URLs.
findings.remediation: HTTP(S)-only validation, DNS/IP policy, redirect revalidation, proxy suppression, HTTPS default.

findings.title: OCR subprocess inherited secrets and lacked output bounds
findings.evidence: Command adapter inherited the complete process environment.
findings.impact: Credential disclosure and local resource exhaustion.
findings.remediation: Executable allowlist, argument array, sanitized environment, fixed cwd, timeout and bounded temporary output.

findings.title: Archive collision and extraction weaknesses
findings.evidence: Duplicate/case-colliding members were accepted and restore used extractall.
findings.impact: Ambiguous overwrite and unsafe restore behavior.
findings.remediation: Reject duplicates, case collisions, links and special files; extract members exclusively.

findings.title: OCR output injection into DOCX and CSV
findings.evidence: Control/bidi characters and formula-leading cells were emitted unchanged.
findings.impact: Document denial of service, UI deception, and spreadsheet formula execution.
findings.remediation: Normalize and sanitize document text; neutralize spreadsheet formulas; verify DOCX relationships.

findings.title: Unbounded rendering page range and projected PDF pixels
findings.remediation: Maximum page count and pre-render projected-pixel rejection.

findings.title: Dynamic SQL field names lacked allowlists
findings.evidence: Attacker-shaped field names reached SQL statement construction.
findings.impact: SQL syntax manipulation if an untrusted dictionary reached the data layer.
findings.remediation: Explicit conversion and attempt column allowlists.

findings.title: Ingestion could read outside manifest source root
findings.evidence: Absolute paths outside the manifest directory were inspected.
findings.impact: Local file disclosure into dataset metadata.
findings.remediation: Require every document, page and ground-truth path to remain under the manifest directory.

findings.title: Deployment exposed broad bind and weak service sandbox defaults
findings.evidence: Deployment scripts did not enforce loopback as a guarded default.
findings.remediation: Loopback defaults, explicit public/auth assertions, systemd sandboxing and restrictive umask.

findings.title: Mutable CI action tags and outdated build backend floor
findings.evidence: Actions used mutable tags and setuptools 65.5.0 had known advisories.
findings.impact: Supply-chain substitution or vulnerable build tooling.
findings.remediation: Pin action SHAs, least permissions/timeouts, setuptools 83 range and reproducible security extras.

findings.title: API lacked early declared body-size rejection
findings.evidence: Oversized Content-Length was not rejected by middleware.
findings.remediation: Fail with 413 before route processing using bounded configuration.

findings.title: Variant secret field names were not redacted
findings.evidence: provider-api-token and db_password_value remained in structured logs.
findings.impact: Sensitive values could appear in diagnostics.

findings.title: Backup and lifecycle symlink handling
findings.evidence: Resolved destinations could obscure a symlink target.
findings.impact: Unexpected restore or archive target selection for a local operator.
findings.remediation: Reject symbolic targets and candidates before mutation.

informational: Bandit emitted 33 contextual warnings: 12 Medium and 21 Low; manual review found no confirmed unresolved vulnerability.
detect-secrets emitted 7 tracked matches; all were synthetic test values or a published fixture checksum.
Semgrep and gitleaks were unavailable and were not claimed as executed.
Public multi-user release remains externally blocked by production identity, TLS and operational decisions.
